name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will be held in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials, and focuses on the themes of Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism, targeting PhD students and young researchers. The programme includes extensive networking opportunities, social activities such as a reception at the Vienna City Hall, and participation from leading experts across AI sub‑disciplines.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
venue: Skeikampen Conference Hotel, near Oslo, Norway
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is organized by the Nordic Language Processing Laboratory and the University of Oslo in Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries and doctoral students, and focuses on multilingual large language models, their evaluation, data curation, and dialectal challenges. The three‑day event takes place at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo and includes technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and a bus transfer from Oslo Airport.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The ProbAI School 2026 is a Probabilistic AI summer school taking place in Vilnius, Lithuania. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state‑of‑the‑art machine learning and artificial intelligence topics. The program welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics and industry professionals to learn and network.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
venue: Durham University, Durham, England, UK
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England, UK. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a wide range of topics in computer vision, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford ML School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 offers participants the chance to upskill in the latest AI theory and applications through three specialised tracks, taught by world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in academia and industry. The programme is hosted by the Oxford ML School and can be attended in Oxford or online, allowing a customisable learning experience where attendees can combine tracks to match their goals. Prospective participants are invited to monitor the timeline for upcoming registration details.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026, organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden, targets doctoral and postdoctoral researchers. It covers a broad spectrum of topics in robotics, perception, and learning, including machine learning, computer vision, human‑robot interaction, planning, and decision making, and combines keynote talks, industry panels, and brainstorming sessions. The six‑day event, held at the Happy Tammsvik resort, provides fully funded accommodation, meals, and activities to foster collaboration and generate concrete research ideas.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
venue: Università di Genova, Genova, Italy
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held in Edinburgh, Scotland, aimed at master’s‑level students and industry practitioners. It offers expert lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art robotic technology. Participants will benefit from networking opportunities, a special dinner and the support of the Edinburgh Centre for Robotics and the UK‑RAS network.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
venue: Hotel Dei Pini, Alghero, Italy
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted by Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering intensive lectures by leading experts, hands‑on sessions, and opportunities for scholarships and networking.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
venue: Porto – Maia, Portugal
description: AIces 2026 is the first international school focusing on the cognitive, ethical and societal dimensions of artificial intelligence. It is aimed at graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering parallel courses, a symposium and open debates. The event takes place in Porto, Portugal from 30 March to 2 April 2026, with both face‑to‑face and live‑online participation and recordings available for 45 days.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
venue: Umeå University, Umeå, Sweden
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems takes place at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session and a social program to foster networking among researchers, students and professionals interested in the societal implications of AI and AS. The three‑day event (11–13 March 2026) includes sessions in Hörsal UB.A.210, Lindellhallen 1 and BIO.E.203, with accommodation recommendations and a registration fee structure for students, faculty and others.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
venue: Escandille, Autrans, France
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) 2026 takes place at the Escandille in Autrans, France. It aims to bring together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human‑robot interaction to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through courses and informal interactions. The program features expert talks on topics like affective signal processing, conversational speech synthesis, and multimodal dialogue, as well as a hands‑on session on human‑robot interaction.

name: Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) Melbourne
venue: Sandridge Events Centre, Port Melbourne, Australia
description: The 2026 Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) takes place in Melbourne, Australia at the Sandridge Events Centre. It offers a two‑week program (2–13 February) for 50 hand‑picked PhD students and early‑career researchers, covering core theory and emerging topics beyond large language models. World‑class speakers from academia and industry present lectures and tutorials on deep learning, generative modeling, reinforcement learning, geometric deep learning and other cutting‑edge areas.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held in Tromsø, Norway. It runs from January 11th to 15th at UiT The Arctic University of Norway and focuses on deep learning methods such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction and speech recognition. The school targets researchers and practitioners who want to learn state‑of‑the‑art techniques and foster collaborations in the field.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
venue: Swostishree Gurukul, Sanobharyang, Kathmandu, Nepal
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals, bringing together over 500 in‑person participants and thousands of online viewers. The school is held at Swostishree Gurukul in Sanobharyang, Kathmandu, Nepal, and aims to disseminate cutting‑edge AI research and foster collaborations across South Asia.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. The program offers lectures and tutorials on machine‑learning research presented by leading experts from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, EPFL and the University of Oxford. It is aimed at students and early‑career researchers in the region who want to deepen their knowledge and network with the AI community.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a 5‑day program offering keynotes, lectures, and practical sessions led by AI experts. It targets doctoral and exceptional master students, academics, and practitioners, emphasizing diversity and inclusion across the Mediterranean region. The next edition will be held in Spain in September 2027, featuring topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, and reinforcement learning.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program held at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It offers young researchers, Ph.D. and master students, post‑docs, and industry professionals a mix of theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, and project mentorship delivered by world‑renowned experts. The school aims to build the next generation of AI leaders in Morocco through expert instruction, networking opportunities, and collaboration with partners such as MoroccoAI, IMT Nord Europe, and Soft Centre.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
venue: NCSR Demokritos, Athens, Greece
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 will be held at the campus of NCSR Demokritos in Athens, Greece. The program brings together experts from RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, and Heriot‑Watt University to provide intensive training in natural language processing. It precedes the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Chania, offering participants a chance to engage with cutting‑edge AI research.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The course focuses on deep unsupervised learning, generative AI, and related techniques. It is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML experience who are considering graduate study or research. The program is offered as a residential or online session from 29 June to 17 July 2026 at Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students and post‑doctoral scholars to explore how artificial intelligence can accelerate scientific research. The program covers machine‑learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops using Python libraries such as NumPy and PyTorch. Emphasizing interdisciplinary collaboration, the school aims to equip participants with practical skills to apply deep‑learning techniques to their own scientific problems.

name: LUT Summer School
venue: LUT University, Lappeenranta, Finland
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive programme offering courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management, inventive thinking and the intersection of business and technology. It is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students who wish to earn 3 ECTS credits while experiencing Finnish summer life on the Lappeenranta campus. The programme combines classroom teaching, workshops, group work and a social programme set against the backdrop of Finland’s natural environment.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
venue: Università di Genova, Via Dodecaneso 35, Genova, Italy
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School, a Machine Learning Crash Course, takes place at the University of Genoa’s DIBRIS/DIMA building in Genova, Italy. It offers an introductory yet comprehensive program on fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms, combining theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions for undergraduate, graduate students and professionals. Attendance is in‑person only, participants receive university credit upon successful completion, and the course is free for UniGe affiliates while others pay a modest fee.
